Title: Deep-Seated Armchair — Domestic Rest
Observations: A deep-seated upholstered armchair positioned within a private interior and shown in active use. Its form supports extended rest and asymmetrical bodily orientation, allowing the occupant to recline and elevate the legs without maintaining formal posture. Unlike display or transitional seating, the chair accommodates duration, privacy, and release, functioning as a domestic structure for sustained comfort rather than regulation.
Caption: Photographic record of deep-seated armchair in active domestic use.

Title: Dining Chairs (Matched Set) — Domestic Displacement
Observations: A matched set of dining chairs positioned outdoors after removal from their intended interior setting. Designed for shared domestic gathering, they remain oriented toward collective use while temporarily suspending it. The arrangement preserves social possibility, but shifts it into a provisional condition between abandonment, pickup, and reassignment.
Caption: Photographic record of dining chairs displaced from domestic interior to curbside exterior.
Map_Label: SS-013 — Dining Chairs (Matched Set) — Domestic Displacement
Header_Subtitle: SS-013 — Dining Chairs (Matched Set) — Domestic Displacement

Title: Informal Armchair Arrangement, Sidewalk
Observations: A pair of upholstered armchairs and a small table are positioned on a rug directly within the sidewalk, forming a temporary domestic zone in public space. The grouping imports the visual and social language of the living room into the street, offering comfort, pause, and conversation where movement would normally dominate. Its placement destabilizes the boundary between private interiority and public thoroughfare, making domestic space briefly visible as an improvised urban condition.
Caption: Photographic record of improvised domestic seating arrangement on sidewalk.
